data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) [overlay]

VERDICT: one coaching finding (non-blocking) + affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this chunk satisfies):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: For a docs deliverable the load-bearing correctness risk is honesty/completeness/non-divergence. The plan's mechanism (author docs covering behavior / config / fail-open / cert semantics / CI pattern, cross-referencing 6f2d WS-DOC) is logic-complete, and AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") explicitly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern 5fd2's round-5 review flagged (presenting the ~50ms fast-claim number as user-facing time-to-first-work while the heavy review runs out-of-band, seconds-to-minutes). The canonical doc already realizes this honestly (docs/plan-review-gate.md §"End-to-end time-to-first-work (honest)", lines 231-240). Sound.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented") rather than a proxy — the ACs map to the topics requiring honest documentation, including the Pass-4 affirmative coach (move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only), consistent with 75a9's advisory/single-turn/reference-by-id design (closed, signed).
- APPROACH SELECTION (alternatives generated, no negative priming): the only structurally-different choice for docs-only work is DUPLICATE-the-workflow-engine-content vs CROSS-REFERENCE it. The plan chose cross-reference ("the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"), the correct call — it avoids a divergence/duplication anti-pattern and respects 6f2d WS-DOC as the single source of truth. Defensible at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, and maintainability; generated alternative discarded (not written into the plan).
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented."

FINDING (G6 — fitness/mechanism for a docs artifact whose stated purpose is honest description):

- [ ] Reconcile the "kill-switch" terminology this doc plan promises against the SHIPPED disable mechanism before authoring, so the docs honestly describe behavior rather than misdescribe it. The What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") and AC#1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)") call the disable a "kill-switch," but the shipped mechanism is an ORDINARY per-project config PREFERENCE explicitly defined as NOT a kill-switch: `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default `false`), where "turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" (src/rebar/config.py:259; docs/plan-review-gate.md:180; 5fd2 SC#4: "an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch"). Documenting a "global"/deploy-time-flavored "kill-switch" when the system ships a per-project preference is precisely the docs-vs-behavior divergence a docs deliverable must avoid (the gate's own round-5 self-review flagged this exact terminology drift: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/final_gate_cycle3.json:312-326). Either (a) document the disable AS the `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` preference with its real default-off / per-project semantics and drop the "kill-switch" framing, or (b) if a genuinely separate global kill-switch is intended, name where it ships — as written the term has no referent in the implementation.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") + §Acceptance Criteria line 1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)"); src/rebar/config.py:259; docs/plan-review-gate.md:176-197; 5fd2 SC#4
  - evidence: plan text says "kill-switch"; shipped mechanism is `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default false), documented as "an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" (config.py:259 comment + plan-review-gate.md:180); 5fd2 SC#4 explicitly "not a deploy-time kill-switch"; 5fd2's own gate run flagged the same docs/behavior terminology divergence (final_gate_cycle3.json:312-326).

ANTI-FP: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no genuine structurally-different "better approach" to coach beyond the duplicate-vs-cross-reference question the plan already handles correctly; G6's alternative-generation requirement is otherwise not-applicable here. The one finding above is a real fitness/honesty defect for an artifact whose stated job is honest description, not a manufactured design choice.
